note: Shared, language-neutral conformance vectors for the declarative request-auth stack (spec section 1.4).
This SAME file is the single source of truth consumed by BOTH the Python (rigging) evaluator
(lib/rigging/tests/test_auth_vectors.py) and finelog's Rust conformance test, so the two engines cannot
drift: it replaces 'two parsers that happen to agree' with 'one contract both must pass'.
Encoding rules so the file stays runnable by both engines:
- Only the layer types finelog also has appear: 'cidr' and 'jwt'. Loopback trust is expressed as an
explicit 'cidr' of 127.0.0.0/8 and ::1/128 (no separate 'loopback'/'anonymous' layer here).
- Semantics are first-match: the first layer that authenticates admits, the first that rejects denies,
and an all-absent walk falls to the default-deny terminal (matched = null).
- The 'jwt' layer's verifier is MOCKED per language (Python injects a TokenVerifier; the Rust engine
verifies natively): request.token is 'valid' (the mock accepts), 'invalid' (present but rejected), or
null (absent). A present-but-invalid token REJECTS (stops the walk with a deny); an absent token is
- request.peer is 'host:port'; expect.matched is the layer 'type' that admitted, or null for a deny.

vectors.name: default_deny_terminal
vectors.description: Nothing matches: an out-of-cidr tokenless peer falls to the default-deny terminal.

vectors.name: cidr_admits_in_network_peer
vectors.description: A direct transport peer inside the trusted cidr is admitted without a token.

vectors.name: cidr_before_jwt_admits_trusted_peer_over_unverifiable_token
vectors.description: Order is load-bearing: a trusted-network peer is admitted by cidr BEFORE the jwt layer would reject a token it cannot verify.

vectors.name: valid_jwt_admits_out_of_cidr_peer
vectors.description: An out-of-network peer presenting a valid jwt is admitted by the jwt layer.

vectors.name: invalid_jwt_out_of_cidr_rejects
vectors.description: A present-but-invalid token is REJECTED (never downgraded to ambient trust): the walk stops with a deny.

vectors.name: jwt_only_absent_token_denies
vectors.description: A jwt-only stack: an absent token is ABSENT and falls to the default-deny terminal.

vectors.name: jwt_only_valid_token_admits
vectors.description: A jwt-only stack admits a valid token.

vectors.name: allow_localhost_admits_loopback
vectors.description: Loopback trust expressed as an explicit cidr: a genuine loopback peer is admitted.

vectors.name: allow_localhost_denies_lan_peer
vectors.description: The loopback-only cidr denies a non-loopback LAN peer.

parse_error_stacks.name: empty_list_lockout
parse_error_stacks.description: An empty stack is a total lockout and MUST raise at parse time (do not rely on omission).

parse_error_stacks.name: unknown_layer_type
parse_error_stacks.description: A 'static' layer is a login-exchange verifier, not a request-chain layer; an unknown type MUST raise at parse time.
